response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable individual-contributor engineer with a broad polyglot background and genuine distributed-systems exposure, but the profile is misaligned with the CTO role on nearly every executive dimension. There is no evidence of engineering-organization leadership (hiring, growing, or structuring teams of 10–30+), no board or executive communication track record, and no fintech-infrastructure depth in credit, payments, or regulatory compliance. His agentic-AI exposure — MCP tooling at re:cinq — began roughly one month before this screening and does not constitute demonstrated production multi-agent system ownership. The gap between 'senior IC' and 'executive technical leader of a regulated fintech AI platform' is too wide to bridge with interview stages alone.
strengths: High-throughput distributed-systems hands-on experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O), providing real production credibility in backend scale
Breadth across the stack (TypeScript/Node, microservices, event-driven design, DevOps) aligns with the technical vocabulary required for the role even if depth at the executive level is absent
Early involvement in agentic-AI developer tooling and MCP frameworks at re:cinq signals genuine curiosity and upside trajectory in the AI-agent space
concerns: Zero engineering-leadership experience: no team management, no hiring track record, no org-scaling history — the hard requirement of growing teams from 10 to 30+ people is entirely unmet
Agentic-AI experience is shallow and brand-new (one month, tooling/frameworks level), far below the 'demonstrated production multi-agent systems' bar the role requires

No fintech-infrastructure depth: RIDE Capital exposure is a single IC role at an investment startup with no indication of credit-system design, payment flows, embedded finance, or multi-jurisdiction regulatory compliance
